title: Mean-imputation dilution defeats leak-corr gate for columns with any NaN
detail: Corr filter mean-imputes NaN cells then computes |corr| vs var_y over ALL rows; computed corr = corr_true*sqrt(1-f) for NaN fraction f. With default forbidden_base_corr_threshold=0.99999, f>2e-5 (~2 NaN rows per 100k) lets an exact y-copy pass; it then wins auto-base MI ranking -> composite built on y-derived base. Pre-vectorisation per-column _safe_corr caught this. Float32 dot accumulation (~1e-6..1e-5 error) compounds vs the 1e-5 threshold band.
fix: Renormalise per column by sqrt(var_y_finite_j/var_y_all) via one masked matmul, or route NaN-bearing columns through scalar _safe_corr (per-pair masking); do corr reductions in float64. Regression test: y-copy with 1% NaN must be dropped.
vnote: Mechanism fully reproduced: mean-impute dilutes corr by sqrt(1-f); f=3e-5 y-copy gives 0.9999885 < 0.99999 and passes; scalar _safe_corr=1.0 would drop. But end-to-end shipped leak NOT demonstrated: degenerate-T specs fail hard eps_mi_gain gate (_fit.py:469-472) and the stepwise route is blocked by D3's NaN bug. P1 now; escalates to P0 if D3 fixed alone - fix together.

title: iter_transform crashes on auto-promoted linear_residual_multi specs
detail: iter_transform extracts only spec.base_column (1-D); for multi-base specs (multi_base_enabled=True by DEFAULT) _linear_residual_multi_forward raises ValueError 'base has 1 columns but fitted alphas has K entries' (linear.py:364). All other consumers (ensemble/__init__.py:439,674,879) already stack (base_column,)+extra_base_columns; this public API was missed. No test covers it.
fix: Build cols=(base_column,)+extra_base_columns; np.column_stack when K>1 and pass the (n,K) matrix to domain_check/forward (both accept 2-D). Add multi-base iter_transform regression test.
vnote: iter_transform (__init__.py:160-170) extracts only spec.base_column; _linear_residual_multi_forward raises ValueError (repro'd, linear.py:364) for K>1. multi_base_enabled=True default + linear_residual in default transforms makes specs reachable. Consumers at ensemble/__init__.py:439/674/879 stack extra_base_columns; export_specs comment documents the bug class. No multi-base iter_transform test exists.

title: Multi-base promotion fits joint OLS on NaN-unfiltered rows; silently dead with lag bases
detail: _pool_arrays/_y_train_local are raw train rows; _linear_residual_multi_fit has no finite masking. K>=2 with NaN: SVD LinAlgError -> cond=inf -> collinear fallback (alphas=0) -> every stepwise trial degenerates -> promotion never fires. K=1 seed with NaN: lstsq raises -> caught -> single-base kept. Lag bases (canonical) have leading NaN in train, so the default-ON, benchmark-validated 83% win is silently lost; final fit at 738 is outside the try and can also poison params.
fix: Mask rows to isfinite(y)&all(isfinite(base_matrix),axis=1) in forward_stepwise._cv_rmse_with_folds and before the final _linear_residual_multi_fit (mirror _linear_residual_multi_domain). Biz-value test: promotion fires on y=b1+b2+eps where b1 has a leading NaN.
vnote: Repro'd: pool arrays raw (_fit.py:351,696); NaN seed -> LinAlgError -> blanket except _fit.py:725 keeps single-base (generic WARN); finite seed + NaN candidate carrying true signal -> cond=inf collinear fallback, never accepted, fully silent; finite control promotes. Lag bases thus disable default-ON promotion. Minor overstatements only: 738 param-poisoning unreachable (accepted bases necessarily finite); seed case warns, not silent.

title: Multi-base domain-violation fallback crashes: np.where broadcasts (n,) mask against (n,K) base
detail: For linear_residual_multi/geomean/pairwise specs, base_arr is (n,K) but domain_ok is (n,); np.where(domain_ok, base_arr, 1.0) raises ValueError whenever any base cell is non-finite. Reproduced: NaN in one base cell + NaN-tolerant inner (LGB/CB-style) -> 'operands could not be broadcast (200,) (200,2)'. The fallback machinery itself crashes in its target scenario.
fix: mask = domain_ok[:, None] if base_arr.ndim == 2 else domain_ok; base_safe = np.where(mask, base_arr, 1.0). Add regression test: multi-base wrapper + NaN base row at predict returns y_train_median fallback, no crash; verify test fails pre-fix.
vnote: Verified: domain_check returns (n,) (linear.py:388 np.all axis=1), _extract_base_matrix returns (n,K); np.where((n,),(n,K),1.0) is a guaranteed broadcast ValueError for K>=2 (K!=n). Fallback branch fires on any non-finite base cell at predict; multi-base wrappers wired in _phase_composite_wrapping:298-309. Crash in the designed fallback path.

title: Per-model wrap builds y-clip/median/t-clip from FULL y (train+val+test); promised end-of-target re-fit never runs
detail: emit_per_model_composite_y_scale_test passes y_train=_y_full_arr (full y) to from_fitted_inner; comment claims the end-of-target pass re-fits the envelope, but _run_composite_target_wrapping:286 skips already-wrapped entries, so y_clip_low/high, y_train_median and t_clip permanently embed test-row y statistics -> holdout contamination + contract violation (docstring: 'Training-row y values').
fix: In _run_composite_target_wrapping, when entry is already a CompositeTargetEstimator, recompute fitted_params_ y_clip/y_train_median/t_clip from _y_train_for_wrap (filtered_train_idx slice) instead of continue; or pass the train slice to the per-model hook. Regression: full-flow wrapper envelope == train-slice-only envelope.
vnote: Verified: hook passes _y_full_arr (full y) at :155-161; y_clip Q001/Q999 + y_train_median + t_clip=10*y_std all derive from it; entry.model mutated at :167; _run_composite_target_wrapping:286 'continue' on isinstance CompositeTargetEstimator, so the promised re-fit never runs. Contract violation vs from_fitted_inner docstring; mild but real holdout contamination of reported test metrics.

title: predict_quantile crashes for every requires_base=False (unary) transform
detail: predict_quantile unconditionally extracts base columns; for unary transforms (cbrt_y/log_y/yeo_johnson_y/quantile_normal_y, all in the DEFAULT discovery list) base_column is unset, _resolve_base_columns() returns (), and _extract_base_matrix raises 'base_columns is empty; multi-base transforms require...'. Reproduced on cbrt_y. _predict_unclipped branches on requires_base; predict_quantile does not.
fix: Mirror _predict_unclipped: when not transform.requires_base, skip extraction and pass np.zeros_like(t_q) to the inverse (unary adapter ignores base). Add unary quantile round-trip regression test.
vnote: Verified: predict_quantile:259-260 extracts base unconditionally; unary transforms have base_column='' -> _resolve_base_columns()==() -> _extract_base_matrix raises 'base_columns is empty' (estimator/__init__.py:161). _predict_unclipped branches on requires_base at :36; predict_quantile lacks the branch. Crash for all requires_base=False transforms.

title: fit() materialises a full copy of X even when zero rows fail domain_check (the common case)
detail: X_valid = self._subset_rows(X, valid) runs unconditionally; with an all-True mask pandas .loc[mask].reset_index() copies every block, polars .filter gathers a new frame, ndarray bool-indexing copies. On the 100+GB frames this wrapper rides, peak RSS doubles for nothing - the exact RAM failure mode CLAUDE.md bans.
fix: X_valid = X if n_invalid == 0 else self._subset_rows(X, valid). Behaviour identical (t_train/sample_weight are ndarrays; inner ignores index). Test with recording stub asserting the same object reaches the inner when all rows valid.
vnote: Verified: _estimator.py:532 unconditional _subset_rows; polars .filter / pandas .loc+reset_index / ndarray bool-index all copy even on all-True mask, contradicting RAM comments at 526-531 and CLAUDE.md CRITICAL rule. Exposure: ensemble OOF refit + direct fit() callers (suite main path uses from_fitted_inner). One-line gate fix.

title: PrePipelinePredictShim._transform swallows ALL exceptions and silently feeds untransformed X to the inner
detail: except Exception: return X. Scaler/imputer output is shape-compatible with raw X, so a linear/MLP inner fitted on scaled features predicts garbage on raw X with NO error when transform fails (e.g. sklearn feature-name/order mismatch at predict). Silently wrong component predictions inside the NNLS stack / OOF refit. Same pattern duplicated in ensemble/__init__.py:48-56.
fix: Stop swallowing: log WARNING + re-raise; if one pd/pl boundary case must be tolerated, catch only that exception type and assert output width matches before fallback. Regression: scaler fitted on named columns + reordered-column predict must not silently pass raw X.
vnote: Verified: post_shim.py:44-48 'except Exception: return X' swallows all transform errors. Comment marks it deliberate (defer to inner's error), but defense fails when raw X is shape-compatible: scaled-feature linear/MLP inner silently predicts garbage inside NNLS stack. Duplicate _transform_via in ensemble/__init__.py:53-63 (finding cited 48-56, minor line drift).

title: Yeo-Johnson inverse produces NaN outside lambda-dependent asymptote
detail: YJ inverse needs t*lam+1>0 (lam<0) / 1+t*(lam-2)>0 (lam>2); no clamp -> np.power(neg, frac)=NaN. Verified: lognormal target fits lam=2.1994, inverse of t_hat=-5.11 -> NaN; np.clip(nan)=nan so y-clip can't save it; wrapper T-clip (med+/-10MAD expanded to obs min/max) can cross the asymptote and t_mad==0 disables it; screening inverse has no T-clip. yeo_johnson_y + both YJ chains are in DEFAULT discovery list.
fix: Clamp power bases in _yj_inverse_numpy/_yj_inverse_numba_kernel/_yj_inverse_scalar: np.maximum(t*lam+1.0, 1e-12) and np.maximum(-t*(2-lam)+1.0, 1e-12) (logratio-style soft-cap). Regression test: lam<0 and lam>2 inverse on out-of-envelope t_hat must be finite.
vnote: Repro: _yj_inverse_numpy(lam=2.1994, t=-5.11)=NaN; lam<0/lam>2 reachable (fit clips to [-2,4]; lognormal fits lam=-0.87, negated lognormal 2.80). T-clip (med+10MAD=2.31) crosses asymptote 1.15 so a passing t_hat=1.73 inverts to NaN; np.clip(nan)=nan; predict() has no isfinite guard. Minor detail off: plain lognormal fits lam<0, not 2.1994 РІР‚вЂќ mechanism unaffected. yeo_johnson_y + both YJ chains are in the default list.

title: linear_residual_multi condition gate scale-variant: mixed-unit independent bases falsely collinear
detail: BKW kappa<30 requires column-equilibrated matrix; code takes SVD cond of centered-but-UNSCALED columns. Verified: independent b1~N(0,1), b2~N(0,1e6) -> cond=1.0e6, collinear_fallback=True, alphas=[0,0] -> transform silently degenerates to T=y-mean(y). No caller checks collinear_fallback; default-ON multi-base auto-promotion (_fit.py:738) hits this directly.
fix: Normalise centered columns to unit L2 norm before SVD (base_centered/col_norms) = BKW scaled condition index, unit-invariant. biz_value test: mixed-scale independent bases must NOT fall back; near-duplicate columns must.
vnote: Repro: independent b1~N(0,1), b2~N(0,1e6) -> cond=9.9e5, collinear_fallback=True, alphas=[0,0]; even scale ratio 40 trips the 30 gate (cond=39.4). linear.py:313 computes col_norms but never equilibrates columns before SVD (BKW requires unit-norm); grep confirms collinear_fallback is set but read by no caller; multi_base_enabled=True default.

title: smoothing_spline_residual s has wrong units: m*std(signal) instead of m*var(noise) -> oversmoothing
detail: scipy UnivariateSpline s targets residual SS; correct scale is m*sigma2_noise. Code uses m*std(yc_avg): wrong units AND wrong quantity. Verified: y=5*sin(b)+N(0,0.1), n=3000 -> s=9990 (proper ~30), residual std 1.825 vs 0.1 noise floor, only 70% of variance absorbed (99.96% absorbable). Transform fails its stated purpose; discovery comparisons systematically pessimistic.
fix: Estimate noise via Rice first-difference on b-sorted y: sigma2=sum(diff(y)^2)/(2(m-1)); set s=m*sigma2. Pass w=sqrt(count) per duplicate-averaged knot. biz_value test: sine target residual std <= 2x noise floor.
vnote: Repro: y=5sin(b)+N(0,0.1), n=3000 -> s=10102 (claim 9990), residual std 1.835 vs 0.1 noise floor, 70.3% variance absorbed (claim 70%). extended.py:359 sets s=m*std(yc_avg); scipy s bounds residual SS so correct scale is m*var(noise) -> residual std ~ sqrt(std(signal)), decoupled from noise. smoothing_spline_residual is in the default discovery list; severity P1 consistent with audit's rating of silent missed-win bugs (cf. D3).

title: sample_weight misaligned with fit rows after group-aware eval carve
detail: _carve_inner_eval_split's group branch returns mask-SCATTERED fit rows (line 285) but all five call sites slice weights as a prefix sw[:len(y_fit)] (431-434, 463-465, 703, 868-871, 902-904). With group_ids+sample_weight (both threaded by the suite), every inner OOF refit trains with weights attached to the wrong rows. Silent: lengths match, no error.
fix: Make _carve_inner_eval_split accept sample_weight and return carved sw_fit (or return fit-row indices/mask) and use it at all five call sites. Regression test: non-uniform weights + group carve, assert estimator received sw[fit_mask].
vnote: Group branch returns mask-SCATTERED fit rows (_carve_inner_eval_split:285 via _slice_rows(X, fit_mask)); all five call sites (431-434, 463-465, 703, 868-871, 902-904) prefix-slice sw[:len(y_fit)]. Suite threads both sample_weight and group_ids (xt_ensemble:321-329, 383-391). Lengths match so it is silent. No test pins alignment.

title: kfold OOF composite branch has no eval-set carve; ES boosters silently dropped
detail: kfold>1 composite path fits inner clones with no eval_set (670) while the raw path (698-707), single-split (863-875) and external (422-438) branches all carve one. Inner clones with early-stopping callbacks raise and are excluded per-component (713-719) - the exact prod pathology in _maybe_pass_sample_weight's docstring. Suite DEFAULT is oof_holdout_source='kfold'.
fix: Mirror the single-split composite path: carve (X_fit,t_fit,X_ev,t_ev) from (X_stack_valid,t_stack) with fold-subset group ids, pass eval_set=. Test: kfold + composite spec + ES inner must survive.
vnote: kfold composite branch fits with no eval_set (line 670) while raw (698-707), single-split composite (863-875) and external (422-438) all carve one. ES inner raises ValueError -> per-component except at 713-719 silently excludes it each fold. oof_holdout_source default IS 'kfold' (config:826), so default-mode ensembles lose every ES composite component.

title: Outer OOF split never group-aware: same-group rows span refit-train and holdout
detail: group_ids only drives the inner ES carve (691-697, 818-823); the outer split is plain shuffled KFold (618-621) or rng.permutation (791-794). On grouped data, within-group memorisation inflates every component's OOF quality, so NNLS/linear-stack weights and the dummy-floor gate run on a leaked surface - the inner split is defended against exactly this, the outer isn't.
fix: When group_ids is not None use GroupKFold in the kfold path and a group-level permutation carve in the single-split path (permute unique groups, accumulate to n_holdout).
vnote: Outer split is plain shuffled KFold (617-618) or rng.permutation (791-794) regardless of group_ids; group_ids only feed the inner ES carve (690-697, 816-823). Same-group rows span refit-train/holdout, inflating the OOF surface that NNLS weights + dummy-floor gate consume. The inner-carve docstring (242-248) itself documents +25% prod impact of group-blind splits.

title: Explicit non-monotone time_ordering silently downgrades to random shuffle
detail: use_time_split only flips when time_ordering is already monotone (777-783); non-monotone (panel frames sorted by group-then-time, or any NaT->NaN at 178) silently becomes a random shuffle with no log - future leaks into refit-train on AR targets, OOF optimistic, weights/gates biased. Suite threads ctx.timestamps so reachable in prod. Tests pin only monotone/no-signal cases.
fix: Stable-argsort time_ordering and take the trailing holdout_frac of the SORTED order (past-train/future-holdout regardless of row order); minimum: WARN that the explicit time signal was ignored. Pin both behaviours.
vnote: Mechanics confirmed: non-monotone OR any-NaT time_ordering fails _is_monotone_nondecreasing (isfinite at 178) -> random shuffle with no log (only the monotone case logs). But the fallback is documented in the docstring ('Otherwise: random shuffle, legacy'), and default kfold mode drops the time signal explicitly with INFO (xt_ensemble:349); only reachable with non-default oof_holdout_source='train_tail' (or external_val fallback). Missing WARN + argsort is a fair improvement; P1 leak overstates default exposure.

title: composite_predictions_as_feature deep-copies the whole pandas frame
detail: out = df.copy() defaults deep=True - full duplication of every column. CLAUDE.md RAM rule (CRITICAL) bans df.copy() on hot paths: frames reach 100+GB, copy doubles peak RAM -> OOM. This helper is the documented composite-x-FE stacking building block on the training frame. Polars branch (71) is fine; pandas pays the copy.
fix: Size-gate per repo convention: <~2GB keep the copy; above, add the column in place with a documented mutation contract (inplace knob + try/finally removal, the mutate-and-restore pattern).
vnote: df.copy() (deep) at feature_stacking.py:73 confirmed. But helper is opt-in: composite_feature_stacking_enabled defaults False and the suite explicitly 'does not auto-attach' (_phase_composite_discovery.py:666-673); one call per attach, docstring contracts non-mutation, and the big-frame convention (polars) takes the cheap with_columns branch. Size-gate fix per repo convention is justified, but P1 'hot path' framing is overstated.

title: Rerank parallelism leaves inner LightGBM n_jobs=-1 uncapped (CPU oversubscription)
detail: _build_tiny_model sets LGBM n_jobs=-1; the n_jobs=1 cap in _one_fold only fires when fold-level tiny_model_n_jobs>1 (default 1). Default tiny_rerank_n_jobs=0 (auto) runs min(n_specs,cpu) specs in parallel threads, each spawning all-core LGBM fits: N x cpu threads. Same 4-8x wallclock pathology the in-fold warning describes, at a level with no cap.
fix: When _rerank_n_jobs>1, build tiny models with n_jobs=max(1, cpu//_rerank_n_jobs) by threading effective outer parallelism into _build_tiny_model/_tiny_cv_rmse_*, mirroring the fold-level cap.
vnote: Verified: _build_tiny_model LGBM n_jobs=-1 (_screening_tiny.py:144); fold-level cap only fires when tiny_model_n_jobs>1 (default 1); config default tiny_rerank_n_jobs=0=auto -> min(n_specs,cpu) threads (_tiny_rerank.py:287-303) each running all-core LGBM fits. Oversubscription real on default path; cap missing at rerank level.

title: Wilcoxon gate mathematically unpassable at default tiny_model_n_seed_repeats=3
detail: One-sided paired Wilcoxon with n=3 diffs has minimum exact p=1/8=0.125 > gate_alpha=0.05, so even a spec winning all 3 seeds is rejected; the len>=3 check admits exactly this case. Config default n_seed_repeats=3; comment recommends 5 but no validator enforces it. Enabling use_wilcoxon_gate with defaults silently rejects ALL specs.
fix: Add model_validator: if use_wilcoxon_gate and 0.5**n_seed_repeats > gate_alpha, raise or warn+skip the Wilcoxon stage. Unit test pinning the minimum-power math.
vnote: Math confirmed: scipy wilcoxon n=3 all-negative diffs gives p=0.125>0.05, so gate is unpassable at default n_seed_repeats=3; no validator. BUT the Wilcoxon block sits inside require_beats_raw_baseline (default False), so it needs TWO opt-ins, and rejections are logged at INFO with p-values РІР‚вЂќ not fully silent. Real validator gap, P2.

title: Multi-seed repeats are exact no-ops under TimeSeriesSplit/GroupKFold
detail: Multiseed wrappers vary only random_state; TSS/GroupKFold ignore it (identical folds every seed) and default families lightgbm(no subsampling)/ridge are seed-invariant, so all 3 repeats are bit-identical: 3x wasted Phase-B wall on every time-aware/grouped spec, and Wilcoxon per-seed arrays are pseudo-replicated (identical diffs treated as independent samples).
fix: Short-circuit to 1 repeat when the effective splitter is deterministic and the family seed-invariant; make the Wilcoxon gate require shuffled-KFold seed variation or fall back to the threshold gate with a log.
vnote: Verified: multiseed wrapper varies only random_state (_screening_tiny.py:433); GroupKFold/TSS ignore it; default families (lightgbm,linear) seed-invariant without subsampling -> 3 identical repeats at default n_seed_repeats=3. Group-aware reranks (wired in prod path) pay 3x on the dominant Phase B. Wilcoxon pseudo-replication part is opt-in. Contradicts audit-notes 'independent across seeds' claim.

title: Raw baseline switches to TSS if ANY base is monotone; non-monotone specs gated against mismatched folds
detail: _any_base_monotone is one global flag: with one monotone base, the raw baseline CV is TimeSeriesSplit for ALL specs, but non-monotone specs were scored with shuffled KFold (optimistic). Threshold raw_baseline*tol comes from TSS (typically higher RMSE) -> gate too lenient for non-monotone specs; cross-spec ranking mixes two fold regimes. 'apples-to-apples' comment only holds when every base is monotone.
fix: Compute raw baseline per fold-scheme (one KFold, one TSS; cached, <=2x cost) and gate each spec against its matching scheme.
vnote: Logic confirmed: single global _any_base_monotone flag (_tiny_rerank.py:443-449) sets TSS for the raw baseline while non-monotone specs were scored with shuffled KFold (per-spec base_t_aware at :207). BUT the whole raw-baseline block requires require_beats_raw_baseline=True (default False) РІР‚вЂќ opt-in, so P2 not P1.

title: Composite tiny-CV scores only domain-valid rows; raw baseline and sibling specs score different row subsets
detail: _tiny_cv_rmse_y_scale drops domain_check-failing rows (up to 30% at min_valid_domain_frac=0.7), usually the hard regime; raw baseline keeps all finite-y rows. Gate compares composite RMSE on an easier subset vs raw on all rows, and spec-vs-spec ranking (default config) compares different populations. Production predict covers ALL rows (median fallback), so deployed RMSE includes rows screening never scored.
fix: Fit on valid rows but score each val fold on ALL its rows, emulating the production median fallback for domain-invalid rows (the code already does this for non-finite inverse output at lines 686-693).
vnote: Code confirmed: _tiny_cv_rmse_y_scale drops domain-invalid rows (:604-609); raw baseline keeps all finite-y rows; min_valid_domain_frac=0.7 allows 30% divergence. BUT the raw-vs-composite gate is opt-in (require_beats_raw_baseline=False default); default-path impact is only cross-spec rank bias when transforms have restrictive domains. P2.

title: fit_stacked pass-2 specs based on _oof_* columns silently trained on all-NaN bases downstream
detail: Pass-2 specs whose base_column is an ephemeral _oof_<spec> column (the feature's headline output) merge into specs_ and reach suite training, where _build_full_column_from_splits (core/_misc_helpers.py:172) silently returns all-NaN for missing columns -> every row domain-invalid -> constant T -> garbage composite target trained with no error whenever use_stacked_discovery=True.
fix: Persist the OOF recipe on the spec and rebuild _oof_* columns via composite_oof_predictions before training; or make _build_full_column_from_splits raise/WARN on fully-missing columns and have fit_stacked drop ephemeral-base pass-2 specs with a log. Add an end-to-end regression test.
vnote: Mechanism confirmed: _oof_* cols exist only in local df_aug; _build_full_column_from_splits silently continues on missing cols (_misc_helpers.py:172) -> all-NaN base -> domain all-False -> _ct_t_full stays ALL-NaN (median impute has no finite train rows), not 'constant T'. All-NaN target ships to per-target loop. Opt-in (use_stacked_discovery=False default, benchmark says keep off). P2.

title: fit_stacked_on_residual pass-2 specs auto-trained against RAW y with residual-fitted params
detail: Docstring calls suite integration 'follow-up scaffolding', but _phase_composite_discovery.py:508 already wires it and line 587+ trains every spec in specs_ by applying forward(raw_y, base, params) with params fit on y - pass1_pred: never-validated targets trained silently. The discovered_on_residual marker (object.__setattr__, non-field on frozen dataclass) is read nowhere; names embed __y_residual__.
fix: Do not merge pass-2 residual specs into the suite-consumed specs_ (return on residual_specs_) until residual-aware training exists, or filter on the marker in the suite and promote it to a real CompositeSpec field. Integration test required.
vnote: Fully confirmed: wired at _phase_composite_discovery.py:508-520; suite :587-637 applies forward(raw _y_arr, base, params) with params fit on y-pass1_pred; discovered_on_residual set via object.__setattr__ (_stacked.py:305) and read nowhere (grep: 2 hits, both in _stacked.py); docstring contradicts wiring. Opt-in flag (default False) -> P2.

title: fit_stacked rebuilds the full train-slice X_train inside the per-spec loop
detail: df.iloc[train_idx].reset_index(drop=True) (or polars mask+filter) executes once per spec in the top_specs loop -- up to 3 identical multi-GB materialisations of the train slice (OOM-grade on 100GB frames). Sibling fit_stacked_on_residual correctly hoists the identical slice out of its loop (lines 209-226).
fix: Move the X_train construction above the for-spec loop (verbatim hoist; loop body only needs the factory closure and composite_oof_predictions).
vnote: Confirmed: X_train slice built inside the top_specs loop (_stacked.py:77-93), invariant per iteration; sibling fit_stacked_on_residual hoists it (:209-226). But rebinding frees the prior copy, so it's <=3 sequential rebuilds + transient 2-slice overlap, not 3x peak RAM; loop capped at stacked_max_pass1_specs=3; opt-in feature. Hoist is the right fix; P2 perf.

title: Forward stepwise has no finite-row masking: NaN-bearing lag bases disable or corrupt multi-base promotion
detail: No finite mask on y/bases and _linear_residual_multi_fit has none either. NaN seed base (lag features = canonical seed, leading NaNs): K=1 lstsq raises LinAlgError -> blanket except at _fit.py:725 skips the whole stepwise. NaN candidate: SVD raises in cond gate -> cond=inf -> collinear_fallback constant predictor -> candidate can never win. Multi-base promotion silently no-ops on autoregressive data.
fix: Build a joint finite mask over y + trial base columns inside _cv_rmse_with_folds (like Phase A's valid mask), and mask before the final _linear_residual_multi_fit at _fit.py:738. Regression test: seed with leading NaNs must still accept an orthogonal second base.
vnote: Confirmed: no finite mask in _cv_rmse_with_folds (forward_stepwise.py:105-144) or _linear_residual_multi_fit (linear.py:285-356); _auto_base_pool stores raw train slices (_fit.py:350). Reproduced: lstsq raises LinAlgError on NaN (K=1 seed -> blanket except _fit.py:725 keeps single-base, WARN logged so not fully silent); K>=2 SVD raise -> cond=inf -> alphas=0 fallback, candidate never wins. Default-ON promotion dead on NaN lag bases. Same root cause as D3 (discovery_core) РІР‚вЂќ dedupe.

title: Dead x_screen_valid copy + ungated prebinned slice per work item
detail: x_screen_valid = x_remaining_matrix[valid_screen] runs for every (base,transform) work item but is NEVER used in the default config (mi_estimator='bin', bootstrap off) РІР‚вЂќ mi_t/mi_y_compare/bootstrap all take the prebinned branch. ~80-200 MB dead copy x ~60 work items = 5-12 GB churn/fit. _x_pb_valid (line 164) also copies the int64 matrix even when valid_screen is all-True.
fix: Move the x_screen_valid gather into the non-prebinned else branch; gate the prebinned slice: _x_pb_valid = _x_prebinned if valid_screen.all() else _x_prebinned[valid_screen] (downstream read-only). Bit-identical. Bench via benchmarks/composite_profile.py discovery workload at 100k-sample shape.
vnote: Mechanism verified: default bin+no-bootstrap (config:309,535) makes _eval.py:162 x_screen_valid dead (only read at 170/188/226, all non-default branches); _x_pb_valid:164 bool-mask always copies. But waste is a minor fraction of the per-item prebinned-MI call (int64 slice+5 passes dominate) РІР‚вЂќ P2 not P1.

title: _mi_to_target_prebinned full int64 matrix slice + per-column gathers without all-true gates
detail: fb_f = feature_binned[finite] boolean-slices the whole (n_rows x n_feat) int64 matrix per call even when finite.all() (typical: y_screen/t_screen finite). ~160 MB/call x ~65 calls/fit РІвЂ°в‚¬ 10 GB churn. Per-column col_b[col_valid]/t_idx[col_valid] (330-331) add two 100k gathers per column even with no -1 sentinel.
fix: fb_f = feature_binned if finite.all() else feature_binned[finite]; per-column skip the gathers when col_valid.all(). Both bit-identical, read-only downstream.
vnote: screening.py:317 fb_f=feature_binned[finite] copies full int64 matrix even when finite.all() (typical: y/t targets finite); 330-331 gather col_b/t_idx per column even when col_valid.all(). Sits inside the dominant prebinned-MI kernel (~65 calls/fit incl. mi_t per work item). Both gates bit-identical.

title: Per-base np.delete matrix copies + mi_y_for_base recomputed per base though decomposable
detail: Per base candidate: np.delete copies of the full float32 (~200 MB) AND int64 prebinned (~400 MB) matrices, then _mi_to_target_prebinned re-bins the same y_screen and recomputes per-column MI for all shared columns. Per-feature MI(x_j,y) is identical across bases РІР‚вЂќ only the excluded column changes. K=3 default wastes ~2/3 of baseline MI wall; scales linearly with hint pools.
fix: Compute per-feature MI vector once (reuse the _mi_per_feature_y_fixed hoist pattern, 1.67x bit-exact precedent); mi_y_for_base = np.mean(np.delete(per_feat_vec, idx)) РІР‚вЂќ bit-identical for mean and sum aggregation. Add exclude_col to MI helpers to drop np.delete entirely (~1.8 GB saved).
vnote: Verified: _fit.py:355-358 np.delete copies f32+int64 matrices per base; 370-373 recomputes _mi_to_target_prebinned(y_screen) per base though per-column MI is base-invariant; _mi_per_feature_y_fixed hoist precedent exists (screening.py:356, used in _auto_base:211). But mi_y is only K=3 of ~65 MI calls + ~1.8GB one-off churn РІР‚вЂќ P2.

title: Default-config thread oversubscription: rerank workers x inner LGBM/XGB n_jobs=-1
detail: tiny_rerank_n_jobs=0 auto-picks up to cpu_count spec threads; each fits boosters built with hardcoded n_jobs=-1 (_screening_tiny.py:144,161). The existing inner cap only fires when fold-level n_jobs>1 (default tiny_model_n_jobs=1), so it never applies: 16 cores -> 256-way thread demand on the dominant phase (~576 booster fits). Same 4-8x blow-up class the file's own warning documents.
fix: When _rerank_n_jobs > 1, plumb inner_n_jobs = max(1, cpu_count() // _rerank_n_jobs) down to _build_tiny_model (mirror the fold-level set_params(n_jobs=1) cap). Measure wallclock before/after on a >=8-core host.
vnote: Verified: tiny_rerank_n_jobs=0 default (config:364) -> auto min(n_specs,cpu) threads (_tiny_rerank.py:287-295,299); inner LGBM/XGB hardcode n_jobs=-1 (_screening_tiny.py:144,161); the set_params(n_jobs=1) cap (294-296,646-648) fires only when fold n_jobs>1, default tiny_model_n_jobs=1 -> never. Real oversubscription on dominant phase.

title: Rerank rebuilds per-base matrices + x_full from df (B+1 full extraction passes)
detail: Per unique base, _build_feature_matrix re-extracts ~all columns from df (full N-row materialisation each, see P6); the raw baseline builds x_full from df again (lines 430-432). B=3 + raw over ~500 cols on a 4M-row frame РІвЂ°в‚¬ 2000 full-column transients (30+ GB churn) for matrices differing by one column. _fit.py:330-359 already uses build-once + np.delete for exactly this.
fix: Build x_full once at the top of _tiny_model_rerank; derive per-base matrices via np.delete(x_full, col_idx, axis=1) (~40 MB at 20k rows) and slice base_screen from it; reuse x_full for the raw baseline. Bit-identical.
vnote: Verified: _tiny_rerank.py:171-184 builds per-unique-base matrices via _build_feature_matrix (full df column extraction each); raw baseline rebuilds x_full at 430-432. _fit.py:334+355 already uses build-once+np.delete for the same need. B+1 redundant extraction passes over a 4M-row frame; np.delete fix bit-identical.

title: _extract_column_array materialises full N-row column when callers keep only a small sample
detail: Polars path get_column(col).to_numpy().astype(f32) allocates the entire column for non-f32 dtypes; callers then slice 20k-100k of 4M+ rows (_build_feature_matrix discovery/__init__.py:368, _tiny_rerank.py:175). ~500 full-column transients per matrix build. Repo precedent: cache.py CACHE-P0-1 replaced the same pattern with col.gather(idx) and measured ~100x.
fix: Add optional rows parameter: polars df.get_column(col).gather(rows).to_numpy().astype(np.float32, copy=False) РІР‚вЂќ O(rows) materialisation; wire through _build_feature_matrix and rerank base pulls. Bit-identical (gather/cast commute).
vnote: Verified: screening.py:64 to_numpy().astype(f32) materialises full N-row column; callers slice to screen sample after (__init__.py:368, _tiny_rerank.py:175,180-182). Repo precedent confirmed: cache.py CACHE-P0-1 (lines 320-324,362,407) replaced same pattern with col.gather(idx). gather-then-cast is value-identical.

title: _safe_abs_corr_all allocates 3 full-matrix temporaries, defeating the leak-corr RAM sampler budget
detail: _filter.py's sampler caps the ONE column_stack alloc at 30% of available RAM, but _safe_abs_corr_all then allocates X_f=X[y_finite] (copies even when all-finite), X_dev=X_f-X_means, and (X_dev*X_dev) РІР‚вЂќ 3x8 GB transients at 4M x 500 f32 against an 8 GB budget; the exact fragmentation-MemoryError class the sampler guards (needed_bytes covers only the stack).
fix: Gate X_f = X if y_finite.all(); exploit sum(y_dev)=0: cov = X_f.T @ y_dev exactly (no centering temp) and var_X via einsum('ij,ij->j') minus n*mean^2 (document conditioning caveat РІР‚вЂќ fine for a |corr| threshold filter). At minimum replace (X_dev*X_dev).sum(0) with einsum. Verify rtol=1e-5 on existing corr-filter tests.
vnote: Verified: _filter.py:79 needed_bytes covers ONLY the column_stack (cap 30% avail, line 38,88); _safe_abs_corr_all (screening.py:142,148-149) then allocates X_f (bool-mask copies even all-finite), X_dev, X_dev*X_dev РІР‚вЂќ 3 extra full-size temps -> peak ~4x the budgeted alloc, defeating the sampler's OOM guard.

title: _row_order_fingerprint hashes EVERY row of the whole polars frame to keep 256 hashes
detail: df.hash_rows().slice(0, 256) is an O(N x C) full-frame scan + N x 8-byte Series alloc, then discards everything past row 256 РІР‚вЂќ multi-second + multi-GB on the 100+ GB frames the package targets, silently undoing the CACHE-P0-1 ~100x data_signature optimisation. Docstring claims 'bounded-cost' (line 139). hash_rows is row-local, so slicing first is digest-identical.
fix: df.slice(0, n_take).hash_rows() РІР‚вЂќ O(256 x C). Add a digest-equality assertion to bench_data_signature.py / a regression test on a fixture frame.
vnote: Verified: cache.py:156 df.hash_rows().slice(0,256) РІР‚вЂќ O(NxC) full-frame hash + N-row u64 alloc per data_signature call (line 235), keeping 256 hashes; docstring:137-138 claims bounded cost. hash_rows is row-local so slice-first is digest-identical. Undermines CACHE-P0-1 gather optimisation on 100+GB target frames.

title: data_signature hashes object-array pointer bytes for string/datetime/cat columns - cache never hits
detail: 3 sites tobytes() object-dtype arrays (cache.py:362-363 polars Utf8 sample, :423 pandas object sample, :164-175 pandas row-order fingerprint) - serializes PyObject* addresses, not content. Verified: polars signature differs WITHIN one process; pandas differs across processes. Prod caller passes ALL columns (_phase_composite_discovery.py:162) so disk cache silently never hits on real frames; except(TypeError,ValueError) fallback never fires.
fix: Encode content, not pointers: '\x1f'.join(str) .encode() or np.asarray(x,dtype='U').tobytes() for sampled strings; polars: Series.gather(idx).hash(); pandas fingerprint: pd.util.hash_pandas_object(df.head(n),index=False).to_numpy().tobytes(). Add S16 cross-process regression test in same change.
vnote: Reproduced: object-array tobytes() hashes PyObject* addresses at cache.py:362/423/164-175. Polars sig differs WITHIN one process (str cols re-materialize); pandas sig differs across processes. (TypeError,ValueError) fallback dead (mixed-head tobytes succeeds). Prod passes ALL cols (core/_phase_composite_discovery.py:162 РІР‚вЂќ note 'core/' in path). Not in audit notes. Cache silently never hits on real frames; fails safe (recompute), so P1 not P0.

title: _row_order_fingerprint runs hash_rows() over the ENTIRE polars frame to fingerprint 256 rows
detail: df.hash_rows().slice(0,n_take) computes a u64 per row of the whole frame (full O(N*C) scan + N*8B alloc), discards all but 256 values - a full extra frame pass per signature on the 100GB-class frames this module optimises for. Verified bit-identical when sliced first (polars 1.41.2).
fix: row_hashes = df.slice(0, n_take).hash_rows().to_numpy() - one line, bit-identical, O(256).
vnote: Reproduced: cache.py:156 df.hash_rows() scans the WHOLE frame + allocs N*8B u64 to keep 256 values; slice-first verified bit-identical (polars 1.41.2), 44x faster at 2M rows, O(N) scaling. In-code 'bounded-cost fingerprint' comment is wrong (only payload is bounded). P1 held: per-signature full pass + multi-GB transient alloc on the 100GB-frame workloads this module targets, paid on every cache lookup.

title: iter_transform crashes on multi-base specs produced by default
detail: iter_transform extracts only spec.base_column and calls forward with a 1-D base; linear_residual_multi specs (auto-promoted by default, multi_base_enabled=True, _fit.py:736-742, stamping extra_base_columns) raise 'base has 1 columns but fitted alphas has 2 entries' (reproduced live). Grouped specs also fail: forward requires groups kwarg (linear.py:526-529). export_specs was fixed for this consumer class; iter_transform was missed.
fix: Build base from (spec.base_column,)+spec.extra_base_columns (2-D when K>1); thread groups for requires_groups transforms or raise a clear NotImplementedError naming the spec. Regression test test_iter_transform_multi_base_spec in test_composite_discovery.py; verify it fails pre-fix.

title: Per-model wrap uses FULL y (train+val+test) for clip envelope + fallback median; promised end-of-target re-fit never happens
detail: emit_per_model_composite_y_scale_test passes y_full (all rows) as y_train to from_fitted_inner, which derives y_train_median (predict fallback) and y_clip bounds from it. Comment claims end-of-target pass re-fits with the precise train slice, but _run_composite_target_wrapping:286-287 'continue's on already-wrapped entries. Test-y extremes leak into reported test metrics AND the saved artifact. Runs unconditionally per composite fit (body:894).
fix: Pass y_full=np.asarray(_y_full)[ctx.filtered_train_idx] at _phase_train_one_target_body.py:894 (ctx has it); additionally re-derive y stats from _y_train_for_wrap on already-wrapped entries in the wrap pass instead of bare continue. Regression test: clip bound == y_train.max(), not y_full.max().

title: CT_ENSEMBLE + lag_predict never built for discovery-skipped targets in multi-target suites
detail: Raw-only synthesis fires only when composite_target_specs is GLOBALLY empty; the ensemble loop iterates only targets with a specs entry. Discovery skip paths (extreme-AR 252-261, auto-skip, row-mismatch, fit exception) continue BEFORE export_specs, so a skipped target B alongside a completed target A gets no lag_predict/dummy-floor/AR1 failsafe - despite discovery:247 promising lag_predict will carry the AR signal.
fix: Per-target fill: synthesize an empty [] spec entry for every regression target with trained models missing from composite_specs_by_target_type, instead of gating on the dict being globally empty (or have every discovery skip path write an empty specs entry).

title: Train-RMSE proxy computed unconditionally (full-train predict per component) then discarded under default honest-OOF
detail: _component_train_rmses runs _comp.predict(filtered_train_df) for EVERY component; with defaults (oof_holdout_frac=0.2, source=kfold) line 518 replaces it with OOF RMSEs. Wrap-pass cache is empty by default (skip_wrap_pass_predict=True), so each component pays a full-train predict (minutes for MLP on multi-M rows) per target, for nothing. Only consumed when OOF fails/disabled or the no-OOF stacking fallback runs.
fix: Compute the train-RMSE proxy lazily - only after the OOF attempt fails or when _oof_frac<=0; both consumers are downstream of the OOF attempt.

title: Default kfold OOF silently drops time ordering: shuffled K-fold on AR data biases stack weights and gates
detail: oof_holdout_source='kfold' (default) sets _time_ordering=None even when ctx.timestamps were threaded for time-aware OOF. Shuffled folds leak temporally adjacent future rows into refits, making component OOF RMSEs optimistic on AR targets; that surface drives NNLS weights, dummy-floor gate, AR1 failsafe and the gate==deploy fallback - they can pick an overfit stack over lag_predict.
fix: When time_ordering is present+monotone, prefer the time-aware path (single trailing-slice holdout, or forward-chaining TimeSeriesSplit OOF in compute_oof_holdout_predictions) instead of silently nulling it; at minimum WARN that timestamps were ignored.
